steps.title: Find the contrast signal
steps.content: The word “Yet” signals that the upcoming statement will be surprising given the earlier descriptions of Merino softness and Navajo‑Churro coarseness.

Choice D captures that surprise by saying the Navajo‑Churro’s coarse outer fiber is paired with an inner fiber that, at its softest, is softer than either the mouflon’s or the Merino’s inner fiber.
The other three either claim uniform softness, an increase in inner‑fiber diameter, or overall greater softness, none of which presents the unexpected contrast introduced by “Yet”.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
